holdings.infoBox: FLSW is an equity ETF with a total of 54 individual holdings. The top holdings are Novartis AG stock at 12.94%, Roche Holding AG at 12.83%, Nestlé at 11.56%, ABB Ltd at 7.55%, and UBS Group AG at 5.67%.

overview.description: The Franklin FTSE Switzerland ETF (FLSW) is an exchange-traded fund that is based on the FTSE Switzerland RIC Capped index, a market-cap-weighted index of Swiss equities, excluding small-caps. FLSW was launched on Feb 6, 2018 and is issued by Franklin Templeton.

data.title: Swiss firms invest $27 billion in US after tariff deal, NZZ am Sonntag reports
data.text: Swiss companies invested $27 billion in the United States between January and April, as Switzerland moves to fulfil a pledge ​to sharply increase investment following a tariff agreement with ‌Washingt...

data.title: Swiss Inflation Holds Steady Ahead of SNB Meeting
data.text: Swiss inflation was unchanged in May, reinforcing the view that the Swiss National Bank is unlikely to raise interest rates later this month.

data.title: Swiss Economy Expands Again
data.text: GDP expanded 0.5% in the first quarter, but the war in Iran threatens to derail Switzerland's recovery in the coming months.

data.title: Swiss Inflation Climbs to Highest Since 2024 as Energy Prices Surge
data.text: Consumer prices were up 0.6% on year, higher than the 0.3% inflation rate in March, as the Iran war sent imported energy costs climbing.

data.title: Trump: I hiked Swiss tariffs because their president was rude to me
data.text: Is it a national-security matter if a foreign leader speaks in a way the president doesn't like?

data.title: US tariffs on Swiss goods cut to 15% in deal struck with Trump administration
data.text: In exchange, Swiss officials committed to series of quotas for imports from the US on a duty-free basis

data.title: US-Switzerland trade deal: tariffs slashed to 15%, boosting pharma and watches
data.text: After months of punishing trade tensions, the US and Switzerland announced a breakthrough deal on Friday that will slash tariffs to 15% from the brutal 39% rate imposed in August. The agreement marks ...

data.title: U.S. And Switzerland Reach Trade Agreement—Lowering Tariffs To 15%
data.text: The U.S. and Switzerland have reached a trade agreement that will lower tariffs on Swiss goods to 15%, American and Swiss officials announced on Friday, after President Donald Trump's levies disrupted...

data.title: U.S. and Switzerland reach trade deal to lower tariffs to 15%
data.text: Switzerland has been subject to one of the highest tariff rates levied on an individual country by the Trump administration.

data.title: U.S. and Switzerland working on a deal to slash 39% tariffs
data.text: The 39% duty was met with dismay, with business leaders saying it was damaging business. Switzerland's key exports include watches, jewelry, machinery, chocolate, and pharmaceutical products.

data.title: Breitling CEO: U.S. tariffs 'terrible news' for Switzerland
data.text: Georges Kern, CEO of Swiss luxury watchmaker, Breitling discusses the impact of U.S. tariff rate of 39%, and explains how consumer sentiment is being affected by political instability across Europe.

data.title: Swatch sells watch lampooning Trump's 39% tariffs on Switzerland
data.text: Swiss watchmaker Swatch has begun selling a special edition watch with the numbers three and nine reversed on its face in a play on the 39% tariffs President Donald Trump imposed on U.S. imports from ...

data.title: Swiss Businesses Fear Being ‘Annihilated' by One of the World's Highest Tariffs
data.text: Goods shipped from the country face a 39% tariff in the U.S., which companies warn will have dire consequences if President Trump cannot be quickly dissuaded.

data.title: Swiss President to Leave DC Without Deal to Lower Tariffs
data.text: Switzerland's president is about to leave Washington without reaching a deal to get President Donald Trump to lower the 39% tariff he put on the country. A delegation led by Karin Keller-Sutter presen...

data.title: Switzerland is in a uniquely difficult position when it comes to tariffs. Here's why
data.text: Switzerland is facing 39% tariffs on goods imported to the U.S. from Aug. 7. The looming duties have caused concerns about the Swiss economy, industry and interest rate outlook.
